programs.title: American Icons
programs.desc: The secret of Twain's success was that he echoed and reflected a longing of all sorts of people. Mark Twain became the most popular voice in America.

programs.title: American Icons
programs.desc: Colonial America had no freedom of religion, the men who signed the Declaration of Independence hated each other. The reason you don’t know those facts? Thomas Jefferson.

programs.title: American Icons
programs.desc: Explore the personal side of the man who brought animated characters to life, revolutionized entertainment, and saw the future as a magical place with many possibilities.

programs.title: JFK: Fact & Fable
programs.desc: Television made John Kennedy the ultimate celebrity during his presidency. However, the JFK we remember is the one his wife, Jackie, created after his death.

programs.title: Presidents In Crisis
programs.desc: Before they led America through national crises, Presidents Abraham Lincoln, Franklin Roosevelt and Lyndon Johnson were all transformed by personal trauma. Historian Doris Kearns Goodwin reveals the all too human men who became legends.

programs.title: The Mighty Atom
programs.desc: This documentary feature chronicles the amazing life story of Joseph Greenstein, “The Mighty Atom – Strongest Man In The World”.
